channel.title: Blog on Disraely Salazar
channel.description: Recent content in Blog on Disraely Salazar

item.title: The Illusion of Anonymity: What a Simple Course Survey Reveals About Data Science Ethics
item.description:
Imagine completing a survey at the end of a training course. The first page tells you your responses are completely anonymous. You feel comfortable being honest — maybe even critical — about the course content, the instructor, the pacing. You hit submit and move on.

Now imagine that the same survey required you to log in with your work username and password before you could even see the first question.

item.title: From Operations to Data Analytics: Why I Made the Switch
item.description:
For most of my career, data was something I worked with, not something I studied. As a purchasing and sales manager, I tracked supplier pricing, monitored margins, resolved discrepancies, and managed budgets that ran into the hundreds of thousands of dollars annually. Every decision I made was driven by numbers — but I was making sense of them manually, through spreadsheets, gut instinct, and experience.

Then I started asking a different question: What if I could do this better?
